figures.caption: Table 3: Full results across methods and merged model counts (Part 2/3, continued).

Table 1: Results when merging m = 2 or m = 5 LLMs. All metrics are normalized to the [0, 1] scale. HellaSwag/BBH/MuSR use acc_norm. MMLU-Pro and GPQA-D are reported as normalized accuracies. Avg is the mean over all five tasks.
Table 4: Per-scale results grouped by method.

Figure 1: Average performance versus the number of merged models m. As m increases (and the merged set becomes more heterogeneous/farther apart), several Euclidean-rule baselines exhibit abrupt collapse around m ≈ 5, remaining in a low-performance regime thereafter. The proposed Karcher merge remains stable across m ∈ {2, . . . , 11} and achieves the best overall performance.

Figure 2: Layerwise diagnostics of activation statistics. Top: mean activation variance across transformer layers. Bottom: effective rank (EffRank) of the activation covariance. Compared with interpolation-based merges (e.g., Lerp and Ties), Karcher merging preserves both variance and effective dimensionality across midto-deep layers, indicating reduced representation collapse.
